# americancloud_vpc_tier (Resource)

A network tier (subnet) within a VPC. `name` and `description` are updatable in place; gateway, netmask, acl_id, and vpc_id are immutable (changing them replaces the tier).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "americancloud_vpc_network" "main" {
  name   = "app-vpc"
  region = "us-west-0"
  cidr   = "10.20.0.0/16"
}

resource "americancloud_vpc_tier" "web" {
  vpc_id  = americancloud_vpc_network.main.id
  name    = "web"
  gateway = "10.20.1.1"
  netmask = "255.255.255.0"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `gateway` (String) Gateway IP for the tier, e.g. `10.0.1.1`. Forces replacement.
- `name` (String) Tier name. Updatable in place.
- `netmask` (String) Netmask for the tier, e.g. `255.255.255.0`. Forces replacement.
- `vpc_id` (String) Parent VPC identifier (from `americancloud_vpc_network`).

### Optional

- `acl_id` (String) Network ACL identifier to apply to the tier. Omit for the default. Forces replacement.
- `description` (String) Optional description. Updatable in place.

### Read-Only

- `cidr` (String) Tier CIDR (derived from gateway + netmask).
- `id` (String) Tier identifier (UUID).
- `status` (String) Tier status.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# VPC tiers are imported as <vpc_id>/<tier_id>.
terraform import americancloud_vpc_tier.web 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111/22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222
```
